close_reason: Requires desktop keychain environment (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, or freedesktop Secret Service). Unit tests cover logic; integration test needs manual environment setup.
description:
secretx-keyring has an env-gated integration test (SECRETX_KEYRING_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1) that round-trips put/get/refresh/NotFound through the kernel keyring (lib.rs:285-335). secretx-desktop has none. This means the documented behavior on macOS, Linux-Secret-Service, and Windows is unverified by CI or by manual run-through.

Impact: regression risk on keyring crate updates. The desktop crate's documented promises (Linux 'returns Unavailable when no daemon' — see issue 1) are unverified. Hard to onboard a contributor who wants to confirm a fix actually works.

Fix: add a SECRETX_DESKTOP_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1-gated #[tokio::test] modeled on secretx-keyring's integration_roundtrip. On Linux it requires a running Secret Service daemon; on macOS/Windows it requires an active user session. Skip cleanly if Unavailable.

This is the right place to also assert the fix for issue 1 (PlatformFailure → Unavailable mapping).
title: No integration test like secretx-keyring has
